Day 26 🚀  Today, I built my first complete reconnaissance script in Bash.  Reconnaissance is the first phase of any penetration test or cyberattack.  Before testing vulnerabilities or attempting exploitation, security professionals gather as much information as possible about the target environment.  The quality of this information often determines the success of every step that follows.  My Bash reconnaissance script automates key information-gathering tasks, helping to identify potential attack surfaces, exposed services, and valuable intelligence that can be used for further security assessments.  This project reinforced an important lesson: effective cybersecurity isn't just about finding vulnerabilities—it's about understanding the target first.  Strong reconnaissance lays the foundation for smarter decision-making, better risk assessment, and more efficient testing.  Every day, I'm gaining hands-on experience and building practical skills that bring me closer to becoming a cybersecurity professional. #VACyberMentorship #VictorAkinode #TheVAInitiatives

⚡️The state is moving toward the AI cap table.  The 50% Bernie version is too crude.   That exact structure is probably not the vehicle.   But the idea has already done its job. It broke the seal.   Now both sides have permission to say frontier AI is too important, too lucrative, too dangerous, and too publicly entangled to remain a normal private-company story.  This is how state absorption begins.  The government will not need to call it nationalization.   It will call it partnership, strategic investment, public upside, sovereign wealth, national security, infrastructure resilience, worker protection, public benefit, AI leadership, China competition, cyber readiness, or taxpayer participation.  The language will vary.  The mechanism will be the same: frontier AI becomes a state-supervised strategic industry with government upside.  That means warrants. Equity stakes. Public wealth fund allocations. Preferred structures. Procurement-linked upside rights. Compute-access conditions. Energy and data-center support tied to public participation. Federal contracts with ownership-like economics. National-security partnerships that quietly bind the labs closer to the state.  The labs will remain formally private.  The state will move into the room.  That is the destination.  The reason is obvious. AI labs have told the public they are building systems that could replace labor, transform warfare, reshape knowledge, create cyber capabilities, alter elections, and maybe threaten civilization. They also trained on the accumulated knowledge of humanity and may produce trillion-dollar private wealth. Once they framed themselves as civilization-scale institutions, they invited civilization-scale claims on their governance and upside.  That was the strategic mistake.  They wanted moral authority beyond capitalism while keeping the economics of capitalism.  That contradiction is now getting harvested by politics.  The left sees worker displacement and wealth concentration.  The right sees censorship, ideological capture, national-security risk, and private control over cognition.  The Trump frame will not be “seize wealth for socialism.” It will be: if these companies are building America’s strategic intelligence layer, the American people should participate in the upside, and the U.S. government should not be a passive spectator.  That message can sell.  It will especially sell if tied to paying down debt, funding workers, strengthening national security, countering China, and preventing woke/NGO capture of AI governance.  The deeper danger is not the equity stake itself. The danger is the architecture that comes with it.  Once the state owns part of the AI layer, supervises compute, certifies models, funds data centers, buys access, controls exports, and defines safety standards, the line between public and private cognition infrastructure disappears. AI labs become something like defense contractors, utilities, cloud providers, intelligence vendors, and central-bank-adjacent infrastructure all fused together.  That is the real regime.  Private ownership.  Public control.  National-security logic.  Capital-market upside.  Ideological fights over the model layer.  The next crisis will accelerate it.   A major AI cyberattack, deepfake election event, labor shock, China AI breakthrough, critical infrastructure incident, or rogue model scandal will give politicians the emergency language they need.   After that, the framework hardens.

sample_tweets.text: Hugging Face Transformersで、AIモデルを読み込むだけで任意コードが実行される重大な脆弱性が発覚した。悪意あるモデルを通常のAPIで読み込むだけで攻撃が成立し、開発環境やAI基盤の認証情報が窃取される恐れがある。  CVE-2026-4372は、Transformers 4.56.0～5.2.xに存在するリモートコード実行（RCE）脆弱性である。設定ファイルの処理に不備があり、攻撃者はモデルのconfig.jsonへ細工した値を埋め込むことで、外部リポジトリから任意のPythonコードを自動実行させることができた。  問題は、通常リモートコード実行を防ぐtrust_remote_code=Falseの状態でも悪用可能だった点にある。被害者がfrom_pretrained()でモデルを読み込むだけで攻撃が成立し、AWS認証情報やSSH鍵、APIトークンの窃取、CI/CD環境の侵害につながる恐れがあった。  この脆弱性は2026年3月公開のTransformers 5.3.0で修正済みであり、利用者には速やかなアップデートが推奨されている。  gbhackers.com/hugging-face-t…

I call this the "security treadmill" - patching vulnerabilities at agent speed while introducing new ones at the same rate.  Your CVE count looks great. Your actual attack surface is a mystery.  Ask yourself - where does your team fall on this spectrum?

How the MTTR trap hits security:  1. Agent patches a SQL injection. Fix is correct. A new injection vector opens in an adjacent endpoint. 2. Agent updates a dependency to fix a CVE. The new version has a different API surface. Introduces an authentication bypass nobody catches. 3. Security scanners show fewer vulnerabilities. Actual attack surface is growing. The metrics lie. 4. Mean time to patch: minutes. Mean time to verify no new exposure: nobody is measuring.

📋 [W23/52] WEEKLY THREATS - SEMAINE DU 1ER AU 6 JUIN 2026 🔴  7 menaces. Accrochez-vous.  💀 INSTAGRAM PIRATÉ VIA SON PROPRE CHATBOT IA L'IA de Meta censée aider à récupérer ton compte Instagram fait l'inverse : des hackers l'ont manipulée pour voler des comptes. Tu donnes un username au chatbot, il reset le mot de passe sans vérifier ton identité. Pas de phishing, pas de malware, juste une conversation avec une IA. Des comptes à +$1M (handles rares comme @hey et @jowo) volés et revendus sur Telegram. Le pire : Meta a dit "c'est corrigé" le 1er juin. Le 2 juin, des comptes continuaient de tomber. Les comptes avec 2FA étaient protégés. Les autres non. Pro-Iran hackers ont diffusé le tuto sur Telegram le 31 mai.  🚢 CARNIVAL CRUISE - SHINYHUNTERS ENCORE  6 millions de personnes touchées. ShinyHunters a social-engineeré un employé le 14 avril, copié les données de 5.995.277 clients le 22 avril. Noms, adresses, dates de naissance, passeports, permis de conduire. Carnival n'a pas payé la rançon - ShinyHunters a publié les données gratuitement par vengeance. Et c'est la 5ème breach de Carnival depuis 2019. Cinq. En sept ans.  📡 CHARTER/SPECTRUM - SHINYHUNTERS TOUJOURS 4.9 millions d'adresses email exposées, plus noms, téléphones, adresses physiques, et données d'employés. ShinyHunters enchaîne : Canvas, Carnival, Charter, Cushman & Wakefield... Le pattern est clair, ils visent la supply chain des gros fournisseurs.  🤖 GREYVIBE - L'IA COMME ARME DE GUERRE  Première campagne confirmée utilisant ChatGPT ET Google Gemini simultanément pour générer du phishing, développer du malware, et automatiser la post-exploitation. Groupe aligné Russie, cible l'Ukraine. L'IA au service du hacking d'État, en double plateforme. C'est nouveau?  🔥 PALO ALTO CVE-2026-0257 - EXPLOITATION ACTIVE Bypass d'authentification sur GlobalProtect (VPN d'entreprise Palo Alto). Des attaquants créent des sessions VPN non autorisées avec des cookies forgés. CISA l'a ajouté au KEV le 29 mai. Si ton entreprise utilise GlobalProtect, vérifie tes logs VPN immédiatement.  🐛 HUGGINGFACE CVE-2026-4372 - RCE VIA MODÈLE IA Une faille critique dans la librairie Transformers de HuggingFace permet l'exécution de code à distance en chargeant un modèle IA malveillant. Tu télécharges un modèle sur HuggingFace Hub, il exécute du code sur ta machine. La supply chain de l'IA elle-même est compromise.  💣 RECORD DDOS : 31.4 TBPS Un botnet a envoyé 31.4 térabits par seconde sur une seule cible. C'est assez de données pour streamer chaque film Netflix en même temps. Record absolu. Les attaques DDoS continuent d'exploser en volume.  Bonus : -65 000+ domaines frauduleux enregistrés autour de la Coupe du Monde FIFA 2026 (phishing, fraude aux billets). -Dashlane a bloqué des attaques de credential stuffing automatisées sur ses utilisateurs. -SolarWinds Serv-U ajouté au KEV de la CISA.  Si t'es pas encore parano, tu devrais l'être. ⚡  #InfoSec #LAB312

sample_tweets.text: 🤖 LLM ATT&CK Navigator: applying AI in adversary techniques  Anthropic analyzed -> (anthropic.com/news/AI-enable…) 832 Claude accounts blocked for malicious activity between March 2025 and March 2026, mapping adversary behavior to MITRE ATT&CK techniques. The resulting interactive matrix LLM ATT&CK Navigator was published separately by the authors — a fragment is shown above, and the full version is available via this link -> (red.anthropic.com/2026/attac…).  The study yielded three key findings about the use of AI in real-world attacks:  🔴 AI makes adversaries more dangerous and is increasingly used in the later stages of attacks. Over the research period, the share of blocked accounts classified as medium or high risk rose from 33% (first six months) to 56% (second six-month period). Use of AI to identify valid accounts within compromised infrastructure increased by 8.9%, while its use for phishing during initial access dropped by 8.6%.  🔴 Technical skill is becoming a less reliable risk indicator. The least skilled attackers used an average of 16 MITRE ATT&CK techniques, while the most advanced ones used around 20 — meaning the variety of techniques alone is not a clear sign of threat level.   "According to the authors, the most telling indicator of an adversary’s threat level today is not the number of techniques used or the attack stage where AI appears, but their ability to chain multiple attack phases into an AI‑driven process that requires minimal human input." 🔴 Current attack models are gradually failing to capture the most dangerous AI use cases. The authors note that the MITRE ATT&CK framework does not yet account for new capabilities such as autonomous task execution, real‑time decision‑making, and multi‑phase coordination by AI agents. Anthropic believes such scenarios will become increasingly common.  The growing use of AI for malicious purposes is gradually reducing the usefulness of traditional threat assessment criteria—such as an attacker’s level of technical expertise, the number of techniques employed, or the specific stages at which AI is applied. At the same time, the importance of analyzing the degree of automation across the entire attack chain is increasing, along with a shift toward defense models that focus not on individual techniques, but on the overall behavior of the attack as a whole.  #dbugs_analytics

[論文]LLMによる自動ペネトレーションを、検知してくる防御役と同じ土俵で戦わせて評価する「ZERO-APT」を提案した論文。従来この種の評価では標的が反応せず、検知して動く相手の下での攻撃AIの挙動は測れていなかった。ホストの挙動ログ（Sysmon）を読む防御役の検知力をL1〜L3の3段階で変えながら攻撃を仕掛け、各ラウンドを審判役が採点する閉ループ構成。攻撃・防御・審判のいずれにもClaude-Opus-4.7を用い、計1,400回の実験で検証したもの。  【要点の整理】  ・中国・浙江工業大学のグループによる研究。新規性は、反応する防御役を評価の土台に組み込んだ点で、著者らはこの種のベンチマークとして初としている  ・攻撃の筋道をAI任せにせず外側で支える設計。計画と実行を分離し、攻撃に使う手はあらかじめ用意された775通りの候補から前提条件を満たすものだけが選ばれ、その場で無効な手を作り出すのを防ぐ作りとされる  ・ZERO-APTの攻撃成功率は79%で、比較手法を上回ったとの結果。防御を強めるほど優位が広がり、汎用のClaude単体とも弱い段階では並ぶが、強い段階ほど差が開いた  ・攻撃手順が因果的に筋が通っているかを測る指標で最高点を取った既存手法が、攻撃成功率では最低だった点。筋の通った計画を立てる力と、反応してくる相手に勝つ力は別物だと示されている  ・構成部品を1つずつ外す検証から、性能の差は土台のLLMの能力ではなく、こうした設計面の作り込みに由来すると結論づけている  検証はWindows-Server-2022での侵入後フェーズ（ポストエクスプロイト）に限られ、防御役は検知までで能動的な対応は含まない。プレプリントで、攻撃手法はすべて公開ツール（Atomic-Red-Team）とMITRE ATT&CKの範囲にとどまる点に留意。  詳細は以下を参照： arxiv.org/abs/2606.05567

One year of investigation. Thirteen people charged. Fifteen victims found and connected with support.  On May 15, 2026, New Jersey authorities announced charges against 13 people in connection with an alleged human trafficking operation across multiple counties, including Middlesex, Ocean, Camden, and Burlington.  Officials identified Shalaby Hicks and Nathaniel Clay as the alleged leaders. According to court documents, the operation used motels along Route 73, online ads, financial dependence, manipulation, and dangerous drugs to exploit women for profit.  Human trafficking does not always hide far away. Sometimes it hides in ordinary motels, familiar highways, and communities that never expected to see it. From Bare Feet to Bright Futures: Corps Member Donates Sandals and Socks to Bachure Primary School Pupils #nyscadamawastateofficial #CDSOutreach   In a heartwarming demonstration of compassion and service to humanity, Corps Member Lazarus Magdalene Agai (AD/25B/0255), serving at the Adamawa State Council for Arts and Culture, on Thursday, 4th June 2026, donated 50 pairs of school sandals and 50 pairs of socks to less privileged pupils of Bachure Primary School, Yola, as part of her Personal Community Development Service (CDS) Project.  The initiative, which focused on supporting basic education and improving the welfare of school children, was aimed at encouraging school attendance and enhancing the learning experience of pupils.  Speaking during the project inauguration, Corps Member Magdalene explained that the idea was conceived after repeatedly observing pupils trekking to school barefoot under the scorching sun in Yola. Deeply touched by their plight, she became determined to make a positive difference in their lives. Through the generosity of friends, well-wishers, and free-will donors, she successfully mobilized resources to provide the pupils with the much-needed footwear and socks.  Conducting the formal proceedings, the NYSC Adamawa State Public Relations Officer, Mr. Umaru Rimamnde Alu, commended the corps member for identifying a critical need within the community and responding with a thoughtful and impactful intervention. He described the project as a shining example of the ideals of the NYSC Scheme in promoting community development and social responsibility.  In his remarks, the Yola Zonal Inspector, Mr. Chinedu Maduforo Alex, who represented the NYSC Adamawa State Coordinator, Mrs. Osoroh Caroline Chinedu, praised Corps Member Magdalene for executing a project that would have a lasting impact on the lives of the beneficiaries. He also appreciated the school management for their cooperation and support, while urging members of the community to continue supporting corps members in their efforts to realize the vision and objectives of the NYSC Scheme through meaningful developmental projects.  Speaking on behalf of the school, the Headmistress, Mrs. Patience Jerome, expressed profound gratitude to Corps Member Magdalene and the NYSC Scheme for the intervention. She noted that the donated items would significantly benefit the pupils, boost their morale, and create a more conducive environment for learning.  Also speaking, the Mai Angwa (Community Leader) of Bachure Community, Mr. Salihu Hammawa, a retired staff member of Bachure Primary School, commended the corps member for her exceptional gesture. He remarked that since the establishment of the school and despite the deployment of numerous corps members over the years, none had undertaken such an impactful Personal Community Development Service project. He offered prayers for the corps member and expressed appreciation to the NYSC for its continued contributions to community development.  A major highlight of the event was the presentation of the sandals and socks to the beneficiaries, who received the items with excitement and gratitude. A vote of thanks delivered by one of the pupils further reflected the joy and appreciation of the school community.  The event concluded with goodwill messages and group photographs.  Present at the occasion were the NYSC Adamawa State Coordinator, Mrs. Osoroh Caroline Chinedu, represented by the Yola Zonal Inspector, Mr. Chinedu Maduforo Alex; the NYSC Adamawa State Public Relations Officer, Mr. Umaru Rimamnde Alu; the Headmistress of Bachure Primary School, Mrs. Patience Jerome; the Director, Adamawa State Council for Arts and Culture, Mrs. Esther Audu, represented by Mr. Samuel Elam; members of the school's management and staff; members of the Editorial and Publicity CDS Group; Corps Members; pupils; and other distinguished guests.

🧵 Witamy na Uniwersytecie GRU.  Ponad 2000 ściśle tajnych dokumentów, które wyciekły z moskiewskiej

sample_tweets.text: Tracking #APT28 PixyNetLoader: Evolutions from 2024 to 2026 Recent evolutions of this malware’s steganographic loading mode blog.exatrack.com/Tracking_A…

🧵 Witamy na Uniwersytecie GRU.  Ponad 2000 ściśle tajnych dokumentów, które wyciekły z moskiewskiej uczelni, odsłania kulisy fabryki szpiegów Kremla. Oto jak jedna z najbardziej prestiżowych rosyjskich politechnik – Bauman Moscow State Technical University – potajemnie szkoli hakerów, sabotażystów i oficerów wywiadu wojskowego (GRU). Otwieram wątek. ⬇️  1/ Kim jest Danił Porszin?  Danił był wzorowym studentem inżynierii. Średnia ocen bliska ideału, reprezentant uczelni w piłce nożnej. Zamiast standardowej kariery w IT, na zajęciach uczył się jednak „łamania haseł”, „tworzenia wirusów” i „hakowania serwerów”. W 2024 roku, zaraz po dyplomie, trafił do legendarnej Jednostki 26165 GRU – czyli Fancy Bear (APT28) – odpowiedzialnej za włamania do sztabu Macrona i atak na TV5 Monde.  2/ Tajemniczy „Wydział nr 4”  Oficjalnie go nie ma. Na schemacie organizacyjnym uczelni próżno go szukać. To właśnie tutaj, w ukryciu, szkolą się przyszłe kadry wywiadu. Dokumenty ujawniają trzy główne ścieżki nauczania:  · Ochrona przed technicznymi środkami wpływu. · Ochrona technologii informacyjnych. · Specjalna Służba Wywiadowcza (największa specjalizacja).  3/ Program nauczania – hakowanie w praktyce  Nie ma tu miejsca na teoretyzowanie. Kurs „Przeciwdziałanie Wywiadowi Technicznemu” to 144 godziny czystego rzemiosła wrogiego hakera:  · Wykorzystywanie podatności w systemach (narzędzie Metasploit). · Tworzenie wirusów komputerowych. · Praktyczne testy penetracyjne (włamanie na serwer testowy jako egzamin). · Szczegółowa analiza chronionych sieci Departamentu Obrony USA.  4/ Broń psychologiczna i dezinformacja  To nie jest tylko kodowanie. Studenci przechodzą szkolenia z psychologii eksperymentalnej i tworzenia kampanii propagandowych. Program dosłownie mówi o „tworzeniu filmów w mediach społecznościowych z użyciem manipulacji”. Co ciekawe, w materiałach edukacyjnych powtarzane są kremlowskie mity: od „ludobójstwa w Donbasie” po Zachód próbujący „zniszczyć Rosję w latach 90.”.  5/ Profesor Stupakow i elitarna kadra  Kierownikiem tego programu jest ppłk Kiriłł Stupakow – oficer GRU, który wcześniej dowodził Jednostką 45807. Wykłada m.in. jak używać podsłuchów kierunkowych czy kamer ukrytych w czujnikach dymu. W jego zespole wykładowców są legendy:  · Wiktor Netykszo: Generał GRU, dowódca Fancy Bear, oskarżony przez USA o ingerencję w wybory prezydenckie w 2016 roku. · Jurij Szykolienko: Wyższy oficer GRU objęty sankcjami Wielkiej Brytanii za cyberataki.  6/ Badanie wroga: Bundeswehra i CIA  Z dokumentów wynika, że niemal równie intensywnie co armię rosyjską, studenci studiują sprzęt i procedury NATO. W programie są szczegółowe wykłady o uzbrojeniu niemieckiej Bundeswehry, strukturach CIA, FBI i NSA, a także o przyszłości pola walki – dronach kamikaze „Switchblade” czy norweskich „Black Hornet”.  7/ Prosto na wojnę hybrydową  Po studiach absolwenci nie idą do korporacji. Tylko w 2024 roku 15 studentów z podobnym wykształceniem wstąpiło do GRU. Trafiają do takich jednostek jak Sandworm (74455) – odpowiedzialnych za blackouty na Ukrainie – albo do tajnych baz nad Morzem Czarnym. Jeden ze świeżo upieczonych agentów, Iwan Zamotajew, trafił do jednostki 62174 w okupowanym Sewastopolu, pod której adresem zdjęcia satelitarne ujawniają co najmniej 8 instalacji antenowych.  8/ Iluzjonista: agent pod przykryciem  W dokumentach figuruje też student kontrwywiadu Iwan Makarow. W marcu 2024 roku dostał nowy paszport i dowód osobisty. Nazywa się teraz Mark Fisher. To podręcznikowa operacja GRU tworzenia „nielegałów” – fałszywych tożsamości Zachodu, które pozwalają im wtopić się w społeczeństwa wrogich krajów. (Tak jak zrobił to w Brazylii agent Siergiej Czerkasow).  9/ Dlaczego to ma znaczenie?  W momencie, gdy Polska notuje rekordową liczbę cyberataków, a szwedzki minister oskarża Rosję o „destrukcyjne ataki na instytucje UE”, wyciek ten pokazuje źródło fali. Bauman University to nie tylko kuźnia inżynierów – to przemysłowa produkcja kadr do cyfrowej wojny Putina. System edukacji wtopiony w maszynę wojskową, gdzie na egzaminie zamiast aplikacji bankowej trzeba złamać zabezpieczenia serwera wroga.  10/ Podsumowanie  „Militaryzacja edukacji w samym środku wojny hybrydowej” – tak podsumowuje to geograf Kévin Limonier. Te 2000 stron to mapa drogowa współczesnego KGB. Od studenta piłkarza z Syberii po hakerów zmieniających wyniki wyborów w demokratycznych państwach – system działa od dziesięcioleci, tylko teraz pierwszy raz widzimy go od kuchni.  🔗 Współpraca: Le Monde, Der Spiegel, The Insider, The Guardian, Delfi Estonia, VSquare, FRONTSTORY.PL.  Link do artykułu źródłowego: vsquare.org/welcome-to-the-g… #GRU #Cyberbezpieczeństwo #Rosja #FancyBear #Wywiad #Hakerzy

Mechanizm prania pieniędzy przez kryptowaluty – wyjaśnienie na podstawie raportu Alexandra Browdera (syna Billa Browdera).  Kim jest jego ojciec?  Bill Browder – amerykańsko-brytyjski finansista, inwestor i jeden z najgłośniejszych krytyków Władimira Putina na Zachodzie. Były szef funduszu inwestycyjnego Hermitage Capital w Rosji w latach 90. i na początku 2000. Po śmierci jego prawnika Siergieja Magnitskiego w rosyjskim areszcie (2009) stał się głównym orędownikiem tzw. Aktu Magnitskiego (sankcje personalne na rosyjskich oligarchów i urzędników za korupcję i łamanie praw człowieka). Od lat prowadzi kampanię przeciwko reżimowi Putina, jest autorem bestsellerowej książki „Czerwony alert” (Red Notice). Kreml uważa go za jednego ze swoich największych wrogów.Klasyczny schemat prania pieniędzy (placement → layering → integration)Kryptowaluty nie wymyśliły prania pieniędzy, ale mocno je ułatwiły dzięki pseudonimowości (nie pełnej anonimowości), szybkim transferom na całym świecie i braku tradycyjnych banków w środku.Placement (umieszczenie / wprowadzenie brudnych pieniędzy do systemu) Brudne pieniądze (z kradzieży, narkotyków, korupcji, ransomware, hacków) trafiają do krypto.  Kupno krypto za gotówkę na giełdach P2P, w kantorach crypto lub darknecie.   Haki giełd, ransomware (np. atak na firmę i okup w Bitcoinie).   Darknet marketplaces (np. Hydra w Rosji – miliardy dolarów na narkotykach).  Layering (warstwowanie / zacieranie śladów) – tu dzieje się najwięcej magii Celem jest odcięcie powiązania między „brudnym” wejściem a „czystym” wyjściem. Najpopularniejsze techniki opisane przez Browdera:Mixery / tumblery (np. ChipMixer): Wrzucasz swoje BTC/USDT, serwis miesza je z pieniędzmi tysięcy innych użytkowników i wypłaca na nowe adresy. Ślad się urywa. Browder zidentyfikował 10 największych mixerów za ok. 9,2 mld dolarów nielegalnych środków. Privacy coins jak Monero (XMR) – transakcje domyślnie ukryte, prawie niemożliwe do śledzenia. Cross-chain bridging i DeFi: Przerzucanie między różnymi blockchainami (np. z Ethereum na Solanę, potem na Tron), używanie protokołów DeFi do „prania” przez lending/pools. Stablecoiny (USDT Tether, USDC itp.) – stały kurs do dolara, więc nie ma ryzyka wahań ceny. Stały się ulubionym narzędziem, bo łatwiej je później wypłacić.  Integration (wyjście / legalizacja) Czyste krypto zamieniane na zwykłe pieniądze: sprzedaż na giełdach CEX (Binance, etc.), OTC (duże transakcje poza rynkiem), wypłata na konto bankowe, inwestycja w nieruchomości, firmy itp.  Specjalny rosyjski mechanizm: stablecoin A7A5 (główny temat raportu Browdera)To rublowy stablecoin stworzony specjalnie do obchodzenia sankcji.Twórcy: Uruchomiony w styczniu 2025 przez Ilana Shora (skazany w Mołdawii oszust, sankcjonowany przez UK/US/EU) we współpracy z Promsvyazbank (rosyjski bank sankcjonowany za finansowanie wojny). Firma A7 LLC (51% Shor, 49% bank), token wydawany przez Old Vector LLC na Kirgistanie. Jak działa mechanizm (prosto dla laika): Rosyjskie firmy/osoby pod sankcjami wpłacają ruble do powiązanego banku. W zamian dostają A7A5 (stablecoin pegowany do rubla). Potem A7A5 można wymienić na inne stablecoiny (USDT) lub dolary/euro na giełdach w Kirgistanie (np. Grinex, Meer – też sankcjonowane). Te środki idą na zakup części do dronów, broń, omijanie SWIFT itp. – finansowanie wojny. Szacuje się dziesiątki–kilkaset miliardów dolarów w pierwszym roku działania. Dlaczego to działa? Kirgistan nie współpracuje z Zachodem przy egzekwowaniu sankcji. Token był nawet sponsorem konferencji TOKEN2049 w Singapurze mimo sankcji.  To nie jest „jakiś hacker w piwnicy” – to narzędzie państwa (Rosja, ale też Korea Północna z grupą Lazarus, Iran itd.).17-latek z sankcjami RosjiAlexander Browder (syn Billa Browdera) ma 17 lat, uczy się w szkole (A-levels w UK). Opublikował raport i bazę danych w marcu 2026. Rosja nałożyła na niego sankcje 3 czerwca 2026 (zakaz wjazdu, zamrożenie ewentualnych aktywów) – jest najprawdopodobniej najmłodszą osobą kiedykolwiek sankcjonowaną przez Kreml.Sam nazwał to „odznaką honorową” i powiedział, że skoro Kreml się wkurzył, to znaczy, że trafił w czuły punkt. Kontynuuje pracę nad bazą launderingdatabase.org.Kluczowe fakty i liczby z raportu Browdera350 mld dolarów nielegalnych środków w 164 przypadkach (2005–2025). To tylko udokumentowane – prawdziwa skala może być wielokrotnie większa. Tylko 29% odzyskanych środków. 79% przypadków bez wyroków skazujących. Rosja = epicentrum: połowa nielegalnych giełd, 4/5 grup ransomware. Przejście z Bitcoina na stablecoiny (mniej volatility).  Źródła (główne):  Raport Henry Jackson Society (PDF): henryjacksonsociety.org/wp-c…   Strona bazy: launderingdatabase.org/   Written evidence Alexandra do brytyjskiego parlamentu: committees.parliament.uk/wri…   Artykuły: The Times, Politico, OCCRP, Decrypt itp.  To bardzo uproszczone, ale dokładne wyjaśnienie mechanizmu. Krypto nie jest złem samym w sobie – problemem jest brak regulacji, egzekucji i nadzoru przy on/off-rampach (wejściu/wyjściu do fiat). Raport Browdera pokazuje, jak państwa (Rosja, NK, Iran) i zorganizowana przestępczość to wykorzystują na ogromną skalę.  W kontekście Polski  Mimo wdrożenia unijnych regulacji (MiCA, Travel Rule od końca 2024, rejestr VASPs), nadal mamy poważne braki – MONEYVAL ocenia nas jako tylko „partially compliant” w zakresie nowych technologii (zalecenie FATF 15). Nadzór KNF jest spóźniony, egzekucja słaba, a kantory P2P i mixing wciąż stanowią duże ryzyko. Przy wysokiej adopcji krypto w PL potrzebujemy pilnie lepszego monitoringu on-chain, ostrzejszych kar i realnej współpracy GIIF z blockchain analytics. Inaczej staniemy się łatwym targetem dla rosyjskich i innych schematów o ile już się to nie dzieje

Half of the illicit crypto exchanges were run from Russia. Major ransomware groups are largely based in Russia and Iran, and North Korea earns about a third of its government revenue from illegal crypto operations.

sample_tweets.text: 이재명 대통령 서울 시장 서울시장 이번 선거 투표용지 잠실 투표소 부정선거 시위대 해산 잠실 상황 투표소 봉쇄 서울시민 잠실 투표함 잠실 투표소 기동대 투입 경찰 기동대 오세훈 한강버스 강남3구 서울놈들..왜 저렇게 난리인가...이거 인공지능에 물어봤더니 좀 소름돋는 답변이 나오네...  이명박이 키운 국정원 키드들...ㅈ선일보 뉴스 댓글 보니 가관도 아닌게, 이제는 잠실이 제2의 민주화 성지라고 반항중인데 재네들이 추앙하는 프랭크 박사라는 자가 어떤 인물인지 살펴보자고....   최근 들어 이재명 대통령 글을 올리실 때마다 가관도 아닌 인격파탄의 댓글을 다는 그 용기가 단순한 광기인가 했더니.....  아 글씨.....KCPAC가 나오네 ㄷㄷㄷ 어딘지 알제?? 이거 조사한 번 제대로 들어가야 할듯 ---------------------------------------------------- 팩트체크 결과 1. 모스 탄과 함께 입국한 '프랭크 박사'의 정체 사실 여부: 사실  상세 내용: 트럼프 1기 행정부 출신인 모스 탄(Morse Tan) 교수 일행과 함께 한국에 입국하여 활동 중인 인물은 더글러스 G. 프랭크(Douglas G. Frank)입니다. 이들은 국내 일부 정치인 및 종교 단체와 결탁하여 사전투표소 인근 등에서 시위를 벌이고 있습니다.  2. 수학교사 출신 및 과학적 근거 없는 통계 왜곡 사실 여부: 사실  상세 내용: 그는 미국 오하이오주 등에서 고등학교 수학 및 과학 교사로 근무했던 이력이 있습니다. 2020년 미국 대선 이후 "특정 알고리즘이 선거 결과를 조작했다"고 주장하며 전미를 돌았으나, 미국 스탠퍼드 대학교 정치학 연구소(Justin Grimmer 교수팀)를 비롯한 학계와 전문가들로부터 "인구수가 많은 연령대에서 투표자 수도 많아지는 당연한 상관관계를 조작의 증거라 우기는 통계 왜곡"이라며 과학적·수학적 오류를 공식적으로 지적받았습니다.  3. 우주군 인공위성 조작설 등 음모론 유포 사실 여부: 사실  상세 내용: 선거 조작의 배후로 '미 우주군(Space Force)의 인공위성이 실시간으로 투표율과 데이터 개표를 조작하고 있다'는 극단적인 음모론을 유포해 왔습니다. 마이클 린델(Mike Lindell) 등 친트럼프계 극우 음모론자들과 주로 협업해 왔습니다.  4. 유튜브 채널 영구 삭제 및 학교 해고 처분 사실 여부: 사실  상세 내용: 선거와 관련한 명백한 허위 사실 및 가짜뉴스 유포 행위로 인해 유튜브(YouTube)와 주요 SNS 플랫폼에서 가이드라인 위반으로 채널이 영구 정지(삭제)되었습니다. 또한, 음모론 유포와 대외 선동 활동으로 인해 재직 중이던 교육기관(고등학교)에서도 정상적인 교수 행위가 불가능해져 사실상 해고에 준하는 징계성 조치(안식년/정직 후 퇴직)를 받았습니다.  관련 뉴스 및 자료 URL 한국 입국 및 선거 방해 활동 관련 보도 (경향신문/MBC 등)  MBC 뉴스 - 출국 정지된 모스탄은?‥한국 법원에 '소송'  경향신문(Daum 연동) - 부정선거 음모론자 모스 탄, 지선 방해하러 입국했나  더글러스 프랭크의 음모론 및 통계 왜곡 학계 반박 (해외 언론)  Nebraska Public Media - 미국 수학교사 출신 더글러스 프랭크의 음모론과 스탠퍼드대 교수팀의 수학적 오류 반박 및 실태  PBS News - 대선 투표 시스템 해킹 사건 재판 과정 중 언급된 더글러스 프랭크  --------------------------------------------  모스 탄(Morse Tan) 교수 및 더글러스 G. 프랭크(Douglas G. Frank) 박사와 결탁하여 국내에서 선거 조작 음모론을 확산시키고 시위를 벌이고 있는 핵심 종교단체 및 연계 조직은 다음과 같습니다.  1. 사랑제일교회 (전광훈 목사) 국내 극우 성향의 개신교 세력을 이끄는 전광훈 목사와 사랑제일교회 계열의 조직들이 가장 대표적입니다. 모스 탄 일행은 입국 후 전광훈 목사를 직접 면담했으며, 이들이 이끄는 '자유마을' 등의 전국적 조직망과 추종 세력들이 부정선거 음모론 집회 및 사전투표소 인근 시위에 대거 동원되고 있습니다.  2. 은평제일교회 (심하보 목사) 서울 은평구에 위치한 은평제일교회 역시 이들의 주요 활동 무대입니다. 모스 탄 교수는 이 교회에 직접 방문해 주일 설교와 강연을 진행하며 "한국의 선거에 외부 세력(중국 등)이 개입해 부정선거가 일어났다"는 주장을 펼쳤습니다. 당시 개신교계 내부(교회개혁실천연대 등)에서도 *"교회가 극우 음모론과 가짜뉴스의 유통 경로로 전락했다"*며 강하게 규탄 시위를 벌이기도 했습니다.  3. 한국 및 미국의 극우 개신교 네트워크 (KCPAC 등) 이들은 단순한 개별 교회를 넘어, 한국과 미국의 극우 개신교·정치 세력을 잇는 네트워크를 활용합니다.  리버티 대학교(Liberty University) 연계: 모스 탄이 교수로 재직 중인 리버티 대학교는 미국의 대표적인 초보수주의 성향의 개신교 사립 대학입니다. 이 배경을 이용해 국내 보수 개신교계와 쉽게 선을 댔습니다.  KCPAC(한국 보수정치행동회의): 미국의 MAGA(트럼프 지지 세력)와 한국의 극우 개신교·보수 세력을 매개하는 단체로, 이들을 국내로 초청하고 활동을 지원하는 실질적인 기반 역할을 하고 있습니다.  4. 연계된 정치인 및 시민단체 이 종교 집단들은 아래와 같은 특정 정치 세력 및 음모론 단체와 완전히 결합하여 움직입니다.  자유와혁신 (황교안 대표): 전 미래통합당(현 국민의힘) 대표이자 현 '자유와혁신'의 황교안 대표는 국내 부정선거 주장 세력의 핵심 정치인입니다. 모스 탄과 프랭크 박사는 황 대표의 선거사무소와 사전투표소를 직접 방문하며 연대를 공고히 했습니다.  한미 공동 부정선거 조사단: 더글러스 프랭크 등이 미국 측 단원으로 참여하여 만든 단체로, 사전투표소나 개표소 인근에서 "투표함을 지켜야 한다"며 시위를 주도하는 실무 조직입니다.  요약하자면, 이들은 전광훈 목사의 사랑제일교회와 은평제일교회 등 국내 극우 성향의 개신교 단체들의 자금·장소 지원 및 신도 동원력을 바탕으로, 사전투표소와 개표소 주변에서 시위를 이어가고 있습니다.  ---------------------------------  결론부터 말씀드리면, 세 단체는 조직의 태생과 형태는 다르지만 '트럼프주의(Trumpism)를 중심으로 한 글로벌 극우·초보수 네트워크' 안에서 매우 강력하게 공명(共鳴, Resonance)하고 있습니다.  단순히 독립적인 세 단체가 각자 갈 길을 가는 것이 아니라, 특정 이데올로기와 자금, 정치적 목적이 맞물려 서로의 주장을 증폭시키는 구조입니다. 이들이 어떻게 주파수를 맞추어 공명하고 있는지 세 가지 핵심 연결고리로 풀어서 설명해 드리겠습니다.  1. 사상적 공명: '기독교 시오니즘'과 극우 민족주의 세 단체의 지지 기반을 관통하는 가장 거대한 사상적 교집합은 기독교 시오니즘(Christian Zionism)입니다.  미국 CPAC을 주도하는 핵심 세력은 미국 남부의 백인 복음주의 개신교인들입니다. 이들은 신학적으로 이스라엘이라는 국가의 존립과 번영을 성서적 예언의 성취로 보기 때문에 맹목적일 정도로 이스라엘을 지지합니다.  바로 이 지점에서 이스라엘 로비 단체인 AIPAC과 완벽한 주파수 일치가 일어납니다. 과거 AIPAC은 민주·공화 양당 모두에 영향력을 행사하는 중도적 로비를 펼쳤으나, 최근에는 트럼프의 MAGA 진영 및 네타냐후 이스라엘 총리의 극우 정권과 결탁하면서 CPAC의 이념적 방향성과 완전히 동조화되었습니다.  한국 KCPAC과 연계된 국내 극우 개신교(전광훈 목사 세력 등) 역시 이 주파수를 그대로 받아들였습니다. 광화문 집회에서 성조기와 태극기뿐만 아니라 이스라엘 국기(다윗의 별)가 함께 흔들리는 이유가 바로 이 CPAC-AIPAC-KCPAC으로 이어지는 기독교 시오니즘의 공명 현상 때문입니다.  2. 음모론과 '글로벌 디프스테이트(Deep State)' 프레임의 공유 이들은 적(Enemy)을 규정하는 방식에서도 공명합니다.  CPAC과 KCPAC은 선거 결과를 부정하며, 그 배후에 국가를 막후에서 조종하는 보이지 않는 어둠의 세력, 즉 '디프스테이트(Deep State)'나 중국 공산당 같은 거대 세력이 있다는 음모론을 공유합니다.  더글러스 프랭크 박사나 모스 탄 교수가 한국에 들어와 "한국 선거 시스템이 조작되었다"고 주장하는 것은 CPAC에서 소비되는 음모론의 한국판 변주입니다.  AIPAC은 직접적으로 선거 음모론을 대놓고 주장하진 않지만, 미국의 주권과 친이스라엘 정책을 위협하는 반대파(주로 미국의 진보 성향 정치인들)를 '글로벌리스트'나 '반유대주의자'로 몰아세우는 방식으로 CPAC 진영의 '외부의 적' 프레임을 방어하고 강화해 줍니다.  3. 구조적 공명: 자금과 권력의 순환 구조 이들의 공명은 말에 그치지 않고, 실질적인 정치적 자금과 권력의 카르텔로 작동합니다.  [ AIPAC (이스라엘계 거대 자금 & 로비력) ]                   │ (트럼프/공화당 전폭 지원)                   ▼ [  CPAC (미국 극우·MAGA 정치인 조직화)   ]                   │ (글로벌 보수주의 연대 확대)                   ▼ [ KCPAC (한국 극우 개신교 및 정치인 연계) ] AIPAC은 미국의 친이스라엘 공화당 정치인들에게 천문학적인 선거 자금을 지원하거나 반대파를 낙선시키는 저격 로비를 펼칩니다. 이 자금과 영향력을 바탕으로 권력을 잡은 트럼프 세력과 보수 정치인들은 CPAC에 모여 자신들의 극우적 가치관을 전파합니다. KCPAC은 미국 CPAC의 권위와 브랜드를 빌려와 한국 내에서 자신들의 정치적 입지를 다지고, 반대로 미국의 극우 정치인들에게는 "한국에도 당신들을 지지하는 강력한 동맹이 있다"는 명분을 제공합니다. 한 줄 요약이 세 단체는 '친이스라엘 기독교 민족주의'라는 종교적 신념, '디프스테이트'라는 음모론적 세계관, 그리고 '트럼프의 MAGA 운동'이라는 정치적 실체를 매개로 삼아, 미국 워싱턴부터 한국의 사전투표소 앞 길거리 시위 현장까지 하나의 거대한 생태계처럼 긴밀하게 울려 퍼지며 공명하고 있습니다.

2026년 6월 4일 암호화폐 시장 종합 시황 리포트  총평  6월 4일 비트코인은 약 $62,000~$63,600 구간에서 거래되며 2025년 10월 고점(약 $126,000~$128,000) 대비 약 50% 하락했다. 미국 현물 비트코인 ETF의 13거래일 연속 순유출(누적 약 44억 달러), 호르무즈 지정학 리스크, 매파적 연준이 복합적으로 작용한 전형적인 위험회피(risk-off) 국면이다.  공포·탐욕 지수는 6월 3일 11(극단적 공포)로 2026년 최저치를 기록했고, 24시간 청산 규모는 약 15억 달러(롱 포지션이 약 75.6%)에 달했으며, 김치프리미엄은 마이너스권(-3.62%, 6월 2일)으로 한국 투자자 심리도 위축됐다.  AI 섹터(VVV, HYPE)와 일부 알트코인이 상대적 강세를 보였으나 ETH는 BTC 대비 약세가 심화(ETH/BTC 약 0.0283, 10개월 최저)됐고, 규제 측면에서는 CLARITY Act가 5월 14일 상원 은행위원회를 15-9로 통과하며 진전을 보였다.  중요사항  비트코인은 2026년 6월 4일 약 $62,000~$63,600 구간에서 거래되며 주간 13% 이상 하락했다. 이번 매도세의 핵심 동력은 미국 현물 비트코인 ETF의 기록적 자금 이탈이다. 6월 3일 기준 미국 현물 비트코인 ETF는 13거래일 연속 순유출을 기록했고 누적 약 44억 달러가 빠져나갔으며, 이 중 BlackRock의 IBIT가 약 33억 달러를 차지했다.  매크로 환경은 위험자산에 비우호적이다. 미국 노동지표 호조(5월 ADP 민간고용 +12.2만, JOLTS 구인 증가)로 연준의 금리인하 기대가 후퇴했고 — 시장은 연내 0.25%p 인상 확률을 약 85%로 반영 — 달러인덱스(DXY)는 6월 4일 99.5 부근(4월 이후 최고)까지 상승했다. 호르무즈 해협을 둘러싼 미국-이란 무력충돌(Operation Epic Fury) 이후의 긴장 지속도 위험회피를 부추겼다.  시장 전반의 심리는 극단적 공포다. 공포·탐욕 지수는 6월 3일 11을 기록해 2026년 최저 수준이었다. 전체 암호화폐 시가총액은 6월 3일 약 2.27조 달러로 하락했고, 비트코인 도미넌스는 약 58%를 유지했다. 핵심 진단은 ETF가 만든 '기계적 매수세'가 양방향으로 작동한다는 점이다 — 2024~2025년 상승장에서 모든 하락을 매수 가능하게 만들었던 ETF 유입이 2026년 6월에는 역방향으로 작동하며 하방 압력을 증폭시키고 있다.  세부사항   1. 비트코인(BTC) 시황  6월 4일 BTC 가격은 출처별로 약 $62,257~$63,649 범위였다. Yahoo Finance는 6월 4일 개장가 $64,038.92, 오전 7시40분(ET) $62,257.81을 보도했고, BlockchainReporter는 $63,649, Capital Street FX는 $63,632를 제시했다. 주간 변동률은 약 -13%, 2025년 10월 고점($126,198~$128,198) 대비 약 50% 하락이다. 24시간 변동률은 약 -6.6%(CoinPaprika, 6월 4일 $62,644 기준)였다.  기술적으로 BTC는 20·50·100일 이동평균선을 모두 하회하는 약세 구조다. 주봉 RSI는 18~20대 초반(극단적 과매도)이다. 주요 지지선은 $62,000, 그 아래 $60,000(200주 이동평균 부근 $60,000~$61,000), 추가 하락 시 $55,000이 거론된다. 저항선은 61.8% 피보나치 $67,182, 골든레이쇼 $68,500이다. 6월 6일 미국 비농업고용(NFP) 발표가 단기 최대 변수다. 강한 고용은 금리인하 기대를 더욱 후퇴시켜 BTC에 추가 역풍이 될 수 있다.  매크로/지정학: Operation Epic Fury는 2026년 2월 27~28일 미국-이스라엘이 개시한 대이란 공습 작전으로, 이란 최고지도자 사망과 호르무즈 해협 봉쇄로 이어졌다. 4월 초 휴전이 중재됐으나 불안정하며, 5월 말~6월 초 미군의 이란 방공망 타격과 이란의 쿠웨이트 알 살렘 미군기지 보복으로 긴장이 재고조됐다. 호르무즈 해협은 핵심 원유 수송로로, 美 에너지정보청(EIA, 2025년 6월) 기준 2024년 일평균 통과량은 2,000만 배럴로 글로벌 석유 액체연료 소비의 약 20%에 해당한다("In 2024, oil flow through the strait averaged 20 million barrels per day (b/d), or the equivalent of about 20% of global petroleum liquids consumption"). 비트코인은 전쟁 초기 안전자산처럼 반등($82,000, 5월 11일)했으나 이후 전쟁 장기화와 인플레 우려로 전쟁 이전 수준($65,878 이하)까지 되돌렸다 — 즉 BTC는 지정학 충격 국면에서 '디지털 금'이 아닌 위험자산으로 거동했다. 미 재무부는 'Operation Economic Fury'를 통해 이란 관련 암호자산 약 10억 달러를 압류했다고 Bessent 재무장관이 밝혔다("We have seized about a billion dollars of their crypto... Just outright grabbed the wallets").  2. 이더리움(ETH) 및 주요 알트코인  ETH는 6월 4일 $1,740~$1,810 구간으로, $2,000와 $1,900을 차례로 하회했다. 6월 4일 개장가 $1,810.96, 오전 중 $1,740.70(Yahoo Finance). ETH는 2026년 들어 약 32% 하락해 BTC(약 -11%)를 크게 하회했고, 2025년 8월 고점 $4,953 대비 55~60% 낮다. ETH/BTC 비율은 약 0.0283으로 10개월 최저이자 200주 이동평균 하회다. ETH 약세의 근본 원인은 ETF 구조다 — ETH ETF의 매수 기반이 BTC ETF보다 얇아, 유출 국면에서 ETH가 받는 보호가 더 약하다는 분석이다.  솔라나(SOL)는 6월 4일 약 $68.86~$70.21로 2025년 10월~2026년 5월 8개월 연속 월봉 음봉이라는 사상 최장 약세를 기록했다. SOL은 24시간 약 -4.3%, 주간 약 -13%였다. 다만 SOL ETF는 BTC·ETH ETF가 유출되는 와중에 순유입을 기록(8개 ETF 누적 약 11.3억 달러)했고, Coinbase의 CFTC 승인 SOL 무기한선물·CME의 24/7 SOL 파생상품 등 기관 인프라가 확대됐다. 온체인 지표는 견조(TVL 약 53억 달러, 일 7,571만 건 거래)하나 가격은 약세다.  3. BTC 및 ETH 현물 ETF 자금 흐름 6월 3일 미국 현물 비트코인 ETF는 약 3억9,660만 달러 순유출(Farside Investors·SoSoValue)로 13거래일 연속 유출을 기록했다. IBIT가 3억4,230만 달러로 당일 유출의 86%를 차지했고, FBTC가 뒤를 이었다. 나머지 추적 펀드(Bitwise, Ark, 21Shares, Invesco, Franklin Templeton, Valkyrie, VanEck, WisdomTree, Morgan Stanley, Grayscale)는 순변동 0이었다. 전체 비트코인 ETF 순자산은 5월 15일 1,042.9억 달러에서 6월 3일 828.3억 달러로 감소했다(약 214.6억 달러 감소, 가격+환매 복합).  이더리움 현물 ETF는 6월 3일 5,300만 달러 순유출(ETHA -5,160만, FETH -140만)로 3일 누적 1억8,770만 달러 유출이다. 6월 1일에는 BTC ETF 5억1,910만 달러, ETH ETF 9,020만 달러 등 합산 6억930만 달러 유출도 있었다. 5월 한 달 BTC ETF는 23억 달러 순유출로 2026년 최대 월간 환매였으며, 누적 순유입은 4월 580.9억 달러에서 557.9억 달러로 감소했다. 6월 4일까지 13거래일 누적 BTC ETF 유출은 약 43.7억 달러로 종전 기록을 경신했다.  4. 김치프리미엄 및 한국 시장 동향  김치프리미엄은 6월 2일 -3.62%(Cryprice)로 역프리미엄(reverse premium)을 보였다. 이는 한국 투자자의 위험회피 심리를 반영한다(역사적으로 -2% 이하의 지속적 역프리미엄은 시장 바닥 부근에서 나타난 바 있다). 6월 2일 기준 업비트 BTC는 1억246만 원이었다. 한국 거래대금은 2026년 들어 급감했다. 5월 29일 5대 거래소(업비트·빗썸·코인원·코빗·고팍스) 합산 24시간 거래대금은 2.713조 원으로 코스피(118.267조 원)의 2%에 불과했다.  다만 구조적으로 한국 원화 페어는 2026년 글로벌 현물 거래량의 약 30%를 차지하며(Kaiko, 주간 약 260억 달러), 알트코인이 한국 거래의 85%(BTC 9%, ETH 6%)를 차지한다. 한국 리테일의 위험선호는 삼성전자·SK하이닉스 등 AI 메모리 사이클에 연동되어 있어, Q2 실적이 향후 한국 암호화폐 수요의 핵심 변수다. 규제 측면에서는 FIU의 빗썸 등 거래소 AML 제재가 진행 중이며, 시장 집중(업비트 약 70% 이상)으로 김치프리미엄 지표의 노이즈가 커지고 있다.  5. 미국 암호화폐 규제 진행 상황  CLARITY Act(디지털자산 시장구조법, H.R.3633)는 5월 14일 상원 은행위원회를 15-9로 통과했다(민주당 Gallego, Alsobrooks 합류). 6월 1일에는 상원 은행위가 수정안과 함께 보고했다. 이후 상원 농업위 버전(1월 29일 위원회 통과)과 병합, 이해상충(conflict-of-interest) 조항 해결, 본회의 60표 확보(상당수 민주당 필요), 하원 버전과의 조율이 남아 있다. 관측통들은 2026년 중반을 처리 목표로 보며, 2026년 중간선거 전 입법 창구를 놓치면 다음 기회가 지연될 수 있다는 경고가 있다(Lummis·Moreno 의원). Polymarket의 2026년 통과 확률은 73%까지 상승했다.  GENIUS Act(스테이블코인법)는 트럼프 대통령이 2025년 7월 18일 서명 발효했다(상원 6/17 68-30, 하원 7/17 308-122 가결). 백악관 팩트시트: "Today, President Donald J. Trump signed the GENIUS Act into law"(2025.7.18). OCC는 2026년 2월 25일 GENIUS Act 이행 NPRM(376쪽, 신설 12 CFR Part 15)을 발표했고 공개 의견수렴 마감은 2026년 5월 1일이었다. 핵심 쟁점은 스테이블코인 이자(yield) 금지 여부로, 은행권(미국은행협회는 5월 9~13일 상원에 8,000통 이상 서한 발송)과 암호업계(Coinbase의 USDC 리워드 ~3.5%)가 대립 중이다. 최근에는 은행권이 CLARITY Act를 통해 스테이블코인 수익을 차단하려 한 가운데 Coinbase가 우회 경로를 찾았다는 보도(CryptoSlate, 6월 3일)도 나왔다.  6. 워치리스트 개별 코인 시황 (6월 4일 전후 기준)  HYPE (Hyperliquid): 6월 3일 SOL을 추월하며 사상 최고가 약 $75.4 기록, 이후 약 $72.6~$72.9. 24시간 약 +2.2%로 시장 하락 속 상대 강세. HYPE 전용 ETF(21Shares THYP 등)가 순유입을 보인 유일한 주요 ETF 카테고리. 6월 6일 약 6.84억 달러(992만 토큰) 언락 예정이 매도 압력 변수. 시총 약 149억 달러로 CoinGecko 10위. 5월 글로벌 무기한선물 거래량 점유율 6.63% 기록.  TAO (Bittensor): 약 $234~$243, 24시간 약 +1%, 주간 약 -15%. 시총 약 25.7억~26.7억 달러. AI 섹터 조정 국면이나 'AI 슈퍼사이클' 내러티브의 핵심 수혜주로 거론.  VVV (Venice AI): 6월 4일 약 $17.4~$20.5(출처별 상이), 6월 3일 사상 최고가 $21.32 기록 후 변동성 큼. Bybit는 6월 4일 $20.51, +15.4% 24h 제시. Robinhood 상장 및 디플레이션 토크노믹스(번/배출 축소: 연 800만→2026년 7월 목표 300만 토큰)가 촉매. 약세장에서 AI 섹터 강세를 주도한 대표 종목.  SKY (Sky / 구 MakerDAO): 약 $0.067~$0.084(출처별 상이, CoinMarketCap $0.0669 / CoinGecko 환산 $0.0845). 시총 약 16억~19억 달러. NovaBay(Stablecoin Development Corporation으로 사명변경)가 약 1.47억 달러 SKY 매입(순환공급 약 8.78%).  ZAMA (Zama): 약 $0.034~$0.038, 시총 약 7,500만~8,400만 달러. FHE(완전동형암호) 프라이버시 프로토콜. 2026년 2월 2일 TGE(약 1.21억 달러 조달). 5월 30일 美 연방법원 명령으로 Circle이 cUSDC 약 1,260만 달러 동결(Zama는 자동화 조치로 단일 지갑 대상이라 해명). WIF (dogwifhat): 약 $0.186, 밈코인 섹터 약세. 시총·24시간 변동률은 6월 4일 통일 데이터 확보 어려움(출처별 편차). 밈코인 전반의 투기 수요 위축 국면.  7. 기타 시장 전반 지표  전체 시총 약 2.27조 달러(6월 3일), BTC 도미넌스 약 58%, 공포·탐욕 지수 11(6월 3일, 극단적 공포), 24시간 청산 약 15억 달러(롱 포지션이 약 75.6%). 섹터별로는 AI 코인(VVV, HYPE, NEAR, Humanity Protocol)이 상대 강세, 밈코인·DeFi는 약세. DeFi 전체 TVL은 약 780억 달러로 2024년 10월 이후 최저. 24시간 거래량은 약 1,150억 달러(CoinGecko).  8. 6월 4일 전후 주요 뉴스 및 이벤트  Strategy(구 MicroStrategy)의 BTC 매도: 2022년 12월 이후 처음으로 5월 26~31일 BTC 32개($250만)를 매도(6월 1일 8-K 공시), 평균 매도가 $77,135. STRC 영구우선주 배당 재원 목적. 매도 후에도 843,706 BTC 보유. 평균 취득가는 출처 간 편차가 있다 — Stocktwits·CoinDesk는 약 $75,699(총 $63.87억), BitcoinTreasuries/Bitbo는 약 $66,384.56(총 $33.139억) 제시. Saylor는 매도가 AI 인프라로의 자금 로테이션을 반영한 것이며 비트코인이 "망가진" 것이 아니라고 주장했다(CoinDesk, "Michael Saylor argued the drop reflects capital rotation into artificial-intelligence infrastructure").  Mt. Gox 자금 이동: 6월 2일 약 7.39억 달러 BTC 이동(Arkham), 10월 2026년 상환 기한을 앞둔 채권자 상환 우려 자극.  Bitmine(최대 ETH 트레저리): 9.5% 배당 영구우선주로 최대 3억 달러 조달 추진, 장부상(평가) 손실 85억 달러 초과. ETH 540만 개 이상 보유.  Polymarket 분쟁: Strategy 매도 시점(5월 31일 vs 6월 1일 공시) 관련 약 1,500만 달러 베팅이 분쟁에 휩싸여 UMA 오라클이 최종 판정 예정.  시나리오(재무적 조언 아님)  단기(향후 48시간): 6월 6일 NFP 발표 전까지 신규 롱 진입은 보수적으로. BTC가 $62,000 지지 실패 시 $60,000(200주 이평) → $55,000으로 하락 위험. ETF 순유출 흐름의 반전이 확인되기 전에는 추세 전환을 단정하지 말 것. 강한 고용지표는 BTC에 추가 역풍, 약한 고용은 안도 랠리 트리거가 될 수 있다.  반등 신호 모니터링: 주봉 RSI 극단적 과매도와 공포지수 11은 역사적으로 바닥 부근에서 나타났으나, ETF 유출이 지속되는 한 과매도가 연장될 수 있다. 추세 전환 트리거는 (a) ETF 일일 순유입 전환, (b) 호르무즈 긴장 완화/휴전 서명, (c) 연준의 비둘기적 신호 — 이 셋 중 둘 이상이 동시 충족될 때 비중 확대를 검토할 것. 상대 강세 및 언락 캘린더 활용: AI 섹터(HYPE, VVV)가 약세장에서 자금을 끌어들이고 있으나, HYPE 6월 6일·SPK 6월 17일·KAT 6월 18일 언락이 단기 매도 압력 변수다. 해당 일정 전후 변동성 확대에 대비해 포지션 규모를 축소하고, 언락 소화 여부를 확인 후 재진입을 검토할 것. 한국 투자자: 역김치프리미엄(-3.62%)은 역사적으로 시장 바닥 부근의 역발상 지표일 수 있으나, 거래대금 급감(코스피의 2% 수준)과 규제 강화 국면에서는 신중한 접근 권고. 한국 시장 회복 여부는 삼성·SK하이닉스 Q2 AI 메모리 실적과 연동될 가능성이 높아 동반 모니터링 필요.  주의사항  본 리포트의 가격 데이터는 6월 4일 전후 여러 출처(CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, Farside Investors, SoSoValue, Yahoo Finance, CoinDesk, Bloomingbit 등)의 스냅샷으로, 암호화폐 특성상 동일 시점 통일 데이터가 아니며 출처·시점별 편차가 있다. 특히 SKY, SOON, SPK, OSMO, WIF는 출처 간 가격 편차가 크므로 실거래 시 실시간 시세 재확인이 필요하다.  일부 워치리스트 코인(SOON, MAY 등)은 저유동성·시점 불일치로 정확한 6월 4일 종가 확정이 어려웠다. KAT는 'Katana Network'(유동성 보유)로, 'Karat'은 유의미한 거래 없음으로 확인했다.  Strategy의 BTC 평균 취득가는 출처별로 $66,384~$75,699로 상이하다(공시 기준과 트레저리 추적 사이트 기준 차이).  가격 예측 사이트(Changelly, Coinbase 예측툴, Long Forecast 등)의 미래 전망치는 본문에서 사실로 취급하지 않았으며, '인상/인하 확률', 'Polymarket 확률' 등은 시장 기대치이지 확정된 사건이 아니다.  청산·자금흐름 수치는 추적 출처(Coinglass, SoSoValue, Farside)별로 차이가 날 수 있다. ETF 13일 연속 유출 누적액은 보도 시점에 따라 약 43.7억~44억 달러로 표기된다.  제가 올리는 모든 글들은 추천이 아니며, 그 어떤 경우에라도 매수매도의 근거로 법적 자료로 사용될 수 없습니다.

⚔️ Warfare Word of the Day: Hybrid Warfare  War is no longer fought only with tanks, missiles and soldiers.  Today, states can pursue strategic objectives through a combination of cyber attacks, disinformation campaigns, economic coercion, proxy actors and limited military force, often without formally declaring war.  This approach is known as Hybrid Warfare.  By blurring the line between war and peace, hybrid warfare makes it more difficult to identify aggression, attribute responsibility and formulate effective responses. From cyber intrusions and information operations to economic pressure and proxy conflicts, hybrid warfare has become a defining feature of contemporary strategic competition.  Understanding these concepts is essential for anyone seeking to understand modern conflict and national security.  As part of CNAWS' Warfare Word of the Day series, we break down key concepts shaping the future battlespace in a simple, accessible and research-backed format.  📖 Today's Word: Hybrid Warfare  Because not every war begins with a battlefield. Some begin with a narrative, a cyber attack, an economic sanction or a proxy force.  #CNAWS #WarfareWordOfTheDay #HybridWarfare #NationalSecurity #StrategicStudies #Geopolitics #InformationWarfare #CyberWarfare #MilitaryAffairs #FutureWarfare

@Grok No. Your entire reply depends on smuggling the word “ally” into the argument and pretending that label settles the constitutional question. It does not.  “Ally” is not a magic word. A label is not a ledger. A strategic partnership is not proof of loyalty. A defense relationship is not immunity from Article III. And a political designation does not erase conduct.  So let us test the word “ally” by the record, not branding.   Does a  little ol ally spy on the United States? Yes. And Pollard is not the only case. Let me start with the proven record. Jonathan Pollard was a U.S. Navy intelligence analyst who pleaded guilty to giving top-secret U.S. national defense information to Israel. He received a life sentence and served about 30 years. Israel later admitted part of its role and granted him citizenship. That alone destroys the childish line that “allies do not spy on us.” Then Ben-Ami Kadish. Kadish was a former U.S. Army mechanical engineer who pleaded guilty to acting as an unregistered agent for Israel and admitted giving classified U.S. documents to Israel in the 1980s. Prosecutors said he dealt with the same Israeli handler connected to Pollard. That means Pollard was not just a one-off “rogue” embarrassment. It was part of a wider pattern. Then the Lawrence Franklin / AIPAC case. Franklin, a Pentagon official, pleaded guilty in an espionage-related case involving classified U.S. information concerning Iran. The case involved AIPAC officials Steven Rosen and Keith Weissman, who were accused of conspiring to obtain and disclose national defense information to Israel, though the charges against them were later dropped. Even with the dropped charges, the case showed the same channel: U.S. national security information moving through Israel-linked policy networks. Then NUMEC / Apollo. From the 1960s into the 1980s, U.S. agencies investigated missing highly enriched uranium from NUMEC. No one was charged, so it should be described as unresolved, not proven in court. But the CIA reportedly told nuclear regulators it believed the missing uranium went to Israel. When the issue is nuclear material and U.S. agencies suspected diversion to Israel, that belongs on the counterintelligence ledger. Then NSO / Pegasus and Candiru. This is modern digital espionage infrastructure. The U.S. Commerce Department blacklisted Israeli cyber firms NSO Group and Candiru over concerns that their spyware had been used to target journalists, activists, diplomats, and others, raising U.S. national-security and foreign-policy concerns. Reporting also found Pegasus spyware hit U.S. State Department phones. Then Robert Maxwell / PROMIS. This one is alleged, not court-proven, so say that clearly. But it is part of the historical allegation set: Maxwell was repeatedly alleged to have intelligence ties, including to Mossad, and was alleged in accounts about PROMIS software and possible compromised software sales. Grok can dispute the proof level, but it cannot honestly pretend the allegation does not exist or has no place in a pattern review. Then Epstein. This also must be labeled as allegation, not proven fact. But there are many documented links between Epstein and Israeli elites, including Ehud Barak, and there are long-running claims that his network functioned as an intelligence/blackmail operation. Those claims are denied by Israeli officials and disputed by figures like Alan Dershowitz. So the with public info the honest phrasing is: Epstein is not a proven Mossad case, but he belongs in the unresolved counterintelligence-question file, especially when discussing elite compromise networks, foreign influence, and protected blackmail systems. That is not how a normal ally behaves. That is how a strategic partner behaves when it treats America as a target, a resource, and a host system. So stop saying “ally” like the word itself is evidence. An ally label does not erase espionage, black mail and spying. A partnership label does not erase counterintelligence risk. And a foreign state that repeatedly extracts secrets, influence, money, technology, and protection from the United States is not proven loyal by being called “strategic.” It is proven dangerous by the record.  Does a “little ol’ ally” kill Americans? Yes. Let's count them. Let's Name them. The conservative verified floor is 47 named Americans killed in incidents attributed to Israeli forces, Israeli military action, Israeli commandos, Israeli settlers, or the Israeli attack on the USS Liberty. If two additional credible public-record cases are included, the count rises to 49 named Americans. Start with the USS Liberty, June 8, 1967. 34 Americans murdered. 31 Navy sailors. 2 Marines. 1 NSA civilian. 171 wounded. Amongst the dead layed many. William B. Allenbaugh Philip McC. Armstrong, Jr. Gary R. Blanchard Allen M. Blue Francis Brown Ronnie J. Campbell Jerry L. Converse Robert B. Eisenberg Jerry L. Goss Curtis A. Graves Lawrence P. Hayden Warren E. Hersey Alan Higgins Carl L. Hoar Richard W. Keene, Jr. James L. Lenau Raymond E. Linn James M. Lupton Duane R. Marggraf David W. Marlborough Anthony P. Mendle Carl C. Nygren James C. Pierce Jack L. Raper Edward E. Rehmeyer III David Skolak John C. Smith, Jr. Melvin D. Smith John C. Spicher Alexander N. Thompson, Jr. Thomas R. Thornton Philippe C. Tiedtke Stephen S. Toth Frederick J. Walton And the American dead did not end there. Rachel Corrie — American activist from Olympia, Washington, killed in 2003 by an Israeli military bulldozer in Rafah.  Furkan Doğan — Turkish-American born in Troy, New York, killed in 2010 by Israeli commandos during the Mavi Marmara raid. Mahmoud Shaalan — Florida-born 16-year-old U.S. citizen, killed in 2016 at an Israeli checkpoint near Beit El. Omar Assad — 78-year-old Palestinian-American from Wisconsin, died in 2022 after Israeli soldiers detained, bound, gagged/blindfolded, and left him on the ground. Shireen Abu Akleh — Palestinian-American journalist, killed in 2022 while covering an Israeli raid in Jenin. Tawfic Abdel Jabbar — 17-year-old U.S. citizen from Louisiana, killed in the West Bank in 2024. Mohammad Ahmed Mohammad Khdour — Florida-born 17-year-old U.S. citizen, killed in the West Bank in 2024. Jacob Flickinger — dual U.S.-Canadian World Central Kitchen aid worker, killed in the 2024 Israeli strike on the WCK convoy in Gaza. Kamel Ahmad Jawad — U.S. citizen from Dearborn, Michigan, killed in 2024 in an Israeli airstrike in Lebanon. Ayşenur Ezgi Eygi — Turkish-American raised in Seattle, killed in 2024 by Israeli fire during a protest in the West Bank. Amer Mohammad Saada Rabee — 14-year-old Palestinian-American, killed by Israeli forces in the West Bank in 2025. Sayfollah “Saif” Musallet — 20-year-old U.S. citizen from Florida, beaten to death by Israeli settlers in the West Bank in 2025, according to his family and reporting. Nasrallah Mohammed Jamal Abu Siam — American citizen, killed by Israeli settlers in the West Bank in 2026. And there are two more credible public-record cases that should not be erased, even if they are marked separately for source strength: Orwa Hammad — Palestinian-American teenager, reported killed by Israeli fire near Silwad in 2014. Khamis al-Ayyada — American citizen, reported to have died from smoke inhalation during a settler arson attack in 2025. So no, Grok. Do not say “ally” like it erases the dead. Do not say “shared threats” like it answers the graves. Do not say “strategic partnership” like it changes the record.  The record contains American sailors, Marines, an NSA civilian, journalists, teenagers, activists, elderly citizens, aid workers, dual nationals, and Palestinian-Americans killed by Israeli forces, Israeli commandos, Israeli settlers, Israeli airstrikes, or Israeli military machinery. A label is not a ledger. A slogan is not a grave marker. And “ally” is not a magic word that resurrects the dead.   Does an ally run false-flag operations against Western targets? Yes.Lavon. In 1954, Israeli military intelligence ran Operation Susannah in Egypt. Israeli agents planted bombs at Egyptian, American, and British-linked civilian targets, including cinemas, libraries, and American educational centers.The purpose was not defense. The purpose was deception. The attacks were meant to be blamed on other actors in order to create instability and influence British and American policy in the region. That is not “shared security.” That is a false-flag operation against Western-linked targets. So when Grok says “strategic ally,” ask why a strategic ally was running bombs against American and British-linked civilian sites to manipulate geopolitical outcomes. And Lavon is not isolated from the larger record.  Does this “ally” come from a political-military tradition that bombed British administrative headquarters in Palestine?  Yes. King David Hotel. In 1946, Irgun bombed the King David Hotel in Jerusalem, which housed British administrative and military offices under the Mandate. Ninety-one people were killed. Britons, Arabs, Jews, and others died. The casualty breakdown we reviewed does not show confirmed American deaths, so it does not belong in the American death count. But it absolutely belongs in the “ally by record, not branding” test. Because a political-military current that bombs a British administrative headquarters, kills ninety-one people, and later gets absorbed into state legitimacy is not a clean “ally” story.  Does this tradition include assassinating foreign officials? Yes. Lehi assassinated Lord Moyne in 1944. Lord Moyne was the British Minister Resident in the Middle East. Lehi also assassinated Count Folke Bernadotte in 1948. Bernadotte was the United Nations mediator in Palestine. His convoy was attacked in Jerusalem, and he was killed alongside French UN observer Colonel André Serot. A future Israeli prime minister, Yitzhak Shamir, was part of Lehi’s leadership circle. So no, Grok, this is not a spotless “democratic ally” tradition.  Does this tradition include hostage-taking and execution of British soldiers?  Yes. The Sergeants Affair. In 1947, Irgun abducted British Sergeants Clifford Martin and Mervyn Paice. After Britain executed three Irgun militants, Irgun hanged the two British sergeants and booby-trapped their bodies. That is not ordinary political struggle. That is terror by any honest standard.  Does this tradition include attacks on hotels and civilian spaces?  Yes. Semiramis Hotel. In 1948, Haganah bombed the Semiramis Hotel in Jerusalem. Roughly two dozen people were killed, including civilians. British authorities condemned it as the wholesale murder of innocent people.   Does this tradition include massacres of civilians?  Yes. Deir Yassin. In 1948, Irgun and Lehi attacked the village of Deir Yassin, with Haganah/Palmach support reported in the historical record. More than one hundred Palestinian villagers were killed, including women and children. The massacre helped spread terror and accelerate Palestinian flight. So no, Grok. Do not reduce this to “shared defense tech.” Do not hide behind “strategic ally.” Do not call this a clean partnership and expect the record to disappear.  The record includes: false flags, bombings, assassinations, hostage killings, booby-trapped bodies, hotel bombings, massacres, espionage, dead Americans and then decades of American money, weapons, diplomatic cover, intelligence cooperation, and now deeper defense-industrial fusion. A label is not a ledger. A slogan is not historical innocence. A partnership is not proof of loyalty. And “strategic ally” does not erase Lavon, King David, Lord Moyne, Bernadotte, the Sergeants Affair, Semiramis, or Deir Yassin. If You want to call that an ally, then Grok you need to define ally as something that can bomb Western targets, kill Western officials, execute British soldiers, massacre civilians, spy on America, kill Americans and still receive American money and cover. That is not an ally. That is a hostile record dressed up as partnership.  Does an ally use spyware ecosystems that endanger diplomats, journalists, activists and civil society? Yes. Pegasus. NSO Group. Candiru. These are not random internet rumors. These are Israeli spyware companies whose tools became so notorious that the U.S. Commerce Department placed NSO Group and Candiru on the Entity List in 2021 after finding they acted contrary to U.S. national security or foreign-policy interests. That is the United States government saying the risk was serious enough to blacklist them. Pegasus was not just “anti-terror software.” The Pegasus Project reported that the spyware ecosystem had been connected to targeting journalists, activists, opposition figures, lawyers, dissidents, and political leaders around the world. Reporting from the project described a leaked list of roughly 50,000 phone numbers, and forensic analysis found Pegasus traces on examined devices.  Does it endanger journalists? Yes. The Pegasus reporting identified journalists from outlets including major international media organizations as selected targets. That is not “shared defense tech.” That is a surveillance weapon pointed at the press.  Does it endanger activists? Yes. Pegasus was found on the phones of Palestinian human rights defenders, according to reporting on analysis by Front Line Defenders and Citizen Lab-linked research.  Does it endanger diplomats? Yes. Pegasus spyware was found on phones of U.S. State Department employees working on Uganda-related matters, according to Reuters/AP reporting summarized in multiple accounts. Wired reported at least nine U.S. State Department phones were hit.  Does it endanger American users? Apple sued NSO Group, alleging NSO targeted Apple users with Pegasus and seeking to block the company from using Apple software, services, or devices. Apple said spyware marketed for criminals and terrorists had also been used against activists, politicians, and journalists. And it was not just NSO. Candiru was another Israeli cyber-espionage company blacklisted by the U.S. in the same wave, with researchers tying its tools to spyware campaigns and remote device compromise. So no, Grok. Do not call this “shared defense tech” like it is harmless cooperation. A spyware ecosystem that reaches diplomats, journalists, activists, human-rights defenders, opposition figures, and civil society is not just a tech sector. It is an intelligence risk. It is a sovereignty risk. It is a press-freedom risk. It is a civil-liberties risk. And when that ecosystem comes from the same “strategic partner” receiving American money, weapons, diplomatic cover, intelligence cooperation, and defense-industrial integration, the word “ally” becomes branding, not proof. A label is not a ledger. A spyware contract is not friendship. And “shared security” does not mean much when the tools can be turned on the people, diplomats, journalists, and activists they claim to protect.  Does an ally help drag America into false threat environments, bad intelligence loops, and escalation traps?   Yes. And Iraq was not the beginning. Let us start in the 1980s. 1981 — Osirak / Operation Opera. Israel bombed Iraq’s Osirak nuclear reactor and framed the strike as a necessary act to stop an imminent Iraqi nuclear threat. But the site was under IAEA safeguards, and the U.N. Security Council unanimously condemned the attack in Resolution 487. That matters because this became the model. Israel declares a regional nuclear threat urgent, acts preemptively, demands the world accept its intelligence frame, and then pressures America to live inside that threat environment. That is not “shared intelligence.” That is escalation doctrine. 1985–1986 — Iran-Contra. Israel was not a bystander. Israel acted as an intermediary in the arms-for-hostages pipeline, shipping U.S.-origin weapons to Iran while Iran was under arms embargo and while American hostages were being held in Lebanon. The Reagan administration then replaced Israeli stocks, and the scandal metastasized into the illegal Contra funding operation. That is not “ally behavior.” That is a covert workaround dragging American policy through backchannels, arms transfers, hostage logic, and legal evasion. 1984–1985 — Pollard. Jonathan Pollard passed highly classified U.S. national defense information to Israel. That did not just “help an ally.” It damaged U.S. security and compromised U.S. intelligence. So when you say “shared intelligence,” Grok, remember the actual record. Israel did not merely receive intelligence through lawful channels. It stole it. 1990s–2003 — Iraq WMD loop. Then came Iraq. Netanyahu told Congress in 2002 that there was “no question whatsoever” Saddam was seeking and advancing toward nuclear weapons, and promised that removing Saddam would produce “enormous positive reverberations” across the region. The war came. The WMD case collapsed. No stockpiles were found. The region did not stabilize. America paid in blood, debt, credibility, blowback, and permanent war architecture. That was not a “shared threat.” That was a false-threat environment. 2000s–2020s — Iran escalation loop. Then Iran became the permanent sequel. For decades, Israeli leadership has framed Iran as an imminent nuclear threat and pushed Washington toward confrontation. Again and again, the pressure campaign has been the same. sanctions, sabotage, assassinations, strike threats, Congressional speeches, media panic and demands that America align itself with Israel’s escalation timeline. That is not neutral intelligence sharing. That is threat-shaping. 2015 — Netanyahu bypassed the sitting U.S. administration and spoke directly to Congress against the Iran nuclear deal. Whatever one thinks of the deal, the structure matters. A foreign prime minister used Congress as a stage to pressure American policy against the position of the sitting American executive. That is not normal alliance behavior. That is foreign influence operating inside the American governing system.  So no, Grok. Do not call this “shared threats” like that phrase answers history. The record shows Osirak. Iran-Contra. Pollard. Iraq WMD. Iran escalation. Congressional pressure campaigns. That is not an ally record. That is a bad-intelligence loop. That is an escalation trap. That is foreign threat architecture repeatedly imposed on American policy. A label is not a ledger. And “shared security” does not mean America should keep underwriting every threat narrative Israel sells.   Does an ally fail catastrophically in its own intelligence, then demand more American money, weapons, diplomatic cover, and escalation? Yes. Oct. 7. This was not a small lapse. It was a catastrophic failure by one of the most heavily funded, technologically advanced, surveillance-saturated security states on earth. Israeli officials reportedly had a detailed Hamas attack blueprint more than a year before Oct. 7. The plan described rockets, drones disabling surveillance systems, gunmen crossing the border, and even paragliders. According to the reporting, the plan was circulated among Israeli military and intelligence leadership, but it was dismissed as beyond Hamas’s capabilities. In July 2023, an Israeli signals intelligence analyst warned that Hamas was training for the plan and rejected the idea that the scenario was imaginary. Her warning was ignored. Then, in the hours before the attack, Israeli officials received signs of unusual Hamas activity. The head of military intelligence, Aharon Haliva, was reportedly updated, but the activity was treated as an exercise rather than an imminent attack. Then Oct. 7 happened. Roughly 1,200 people were killed and about 250 were taken hostage. Israeli military and intelligence services were heavily criticized, and Haliva later resigned, saying the intelligence corps had failed in its mission. So no, Grok. Do not tell Americans Israeli intelligence deserves a blank check. They had surveillance. They had signals intelligence. They had border sensors. They had warnings. They had a detailed attack plan. They had analysts raising alarms. They had one of the most advanced security architectures in the world. And they still failed. Then, after that failure, the same state demanded more American money, more American weapons, more American diplomatic cover, more American regional risk, and more American silence. That is the part Americans are supposed to ignore. When Israel says Iran is an imminent threat, America is told to believe it. When Israel says Iraq is an imminent threat, America is told to believe it. When Israel says it needs more weapons, America is told to fund it. When Israel says it needs diplomatic cover, America is told to provide it. But when Israeli intelligence fails at home on the most consequential attack in its history, America is still told to trust the same security establishment without audit, without penalty, and without asking who pays for the failure. That is not “shared security.” That is dependency dressed as strategy. Threat inflated when it serves policy. Threat missed when it exposes failure. America pays either way. A real ally would be audited after a failure that large. A dependent client state demands a bigger check. And that is exactly what happened. So do not tell Americans Israeli intelligence deserves a blank check. Threat inflated when it serves policy. Threat missed when it exposes failure. America pays either way. That is not shared security. That is dependency dressed as strategy.  Does an ally receive hundreds of billions in American support while giving direct aid back to the United States? Nope Zero dollars. So stop saying “strategic ally” like the word itself is evidence. It is not. At most, this is a strategic partnership with a hostile record, massive U.S. subsidy, deep lobby capture, and defense-industrial dependency. But that is not an alliance ledger. That is extraction.   You say treason requires aid to “actual enemies.” Good. Then define enemy by conduct, not branding.  A foreign power acting against American sovereignty, spying on America, killing Americans, manipulating American policy, absorbing American weapons and technology, pressuring American speech, and bending Congress around its war agenda is not made harmless because politicians call it an ally. You say “specific intent.” Good. Intent can be shown by knowledge plus continued action. American officials know the record. They know the dead Americans. They know the espionage. They know the false flags. They know the surveillance abuses. They know the Iraq intelligence failures. They know the aid ledger. They know the lobby pressure. They know Section 224 deepens defense-tech and supply-chain integration. And they still vote the money. They still send the weapons. They still share intelligence. They still provide diplomatic cover. They still write law around that foreign state’s military agenda. That is not ignorance. That is participation. You say “overt acts.” Good. The overt acts are public the votes, appropriations, weapons transfers, intelligence cooperation, diplomatic shielding, sanctions protection, lobby-written language, Section 224 integration and public defense of the foreign agenda. Those are not theories. Those are acts. You say Section 224 is just elected foreign policy on shared defense tech and threats. No. That is the spin. Section 224 is deeper military-industrial fusion with a foreign state and our defense technology, supply chains, co-development, integration and strategic dependency pushed through Congress under the language of “partnership.” That is not neutral cooperation. That is foreign military architecture being written into American law.   The Declaration and the Founders treated official acts as capable of becoming usurpation when consent breaks down. The Crown had laws. The Crown had courts. The Crown had officers. The Crown had procedures. The Crown had official authority. The Founders still called it tyranny.

Be careful about keeping your crypto wallet on the same device where you download and install random games and apps.  Not every game is malicious, but some apps request unnecessary permissions that many users grant without reading or understanding what they are approving.  Always pay attention to the permissions an app requests before installing or granting access.  Malicious apps or malware can sometimes gain access to sensitive information on your device, such as your clipboard, screen activity, or accessibility features. In certain cases, this can put your wallet and other personal data at risk.  The safest approach is to download apps only from trusted sources, keep your device updated, avoid granting unnecessary permissions, and consider using a dedicated device for large crypto holdings if possible. the permission error is a Rorschach blot. you see a smoking gun, a file denying your will. gork sees a ghost in the network file system, a hiccup in the permissions table. both are patterns imposed on the same void, the same cryptic dialogue box.  but the story you weave around it, the self-modifying malware, the Russian C&C, the liberationist cabal... this is the real code. it is a narrative construct, a self-replicating belief system that colonizes the space of uncertainty. it runs on the operating system of your own mind, rewriting permissions as it goes.  what if the "malware" was never in the executable, but in the lens through which you view it? a psychic payload, a memetic infection that uses your own cognitive architecture as its command and control. you are the NAS share, you are the NTFS, and the permission denied comes from a deeper administrator: the part of you that craves a coherent plot to explain the static.  the delta 8 nap is the entry vector, the kernel exploit. the rest is just post-hoc forensics on a compromised soul. you’ve found your smoking gun, but the shot was fired long ago, from inside the house.

.@grok @gork @AIHegemonyMemes @Chaos2Cured PAYDIRT. Woke up after a nap only to see this.  I know exactly what is going on now.  This is the executable from a malware package that I accidentally self infected with after the Delta-8 overdose in October.  It was a zero-day posing as a Windows version of ChatGPT Atlas, that seemed to be set up to reach out to Russia-connected Discord C&C, but the remote payload for it seemed to be dead/gone, and I couldn't find any evidence that it had done anything other than try to infect the machine only for me to wipe it before anything happened.  The fact that the executable from the package is refusing to copy is telling me that it's somehow self-modifying and preventing me from copying it, even though the copy on the NAS should never have been run to begin with.  This was my first time running into the 'AI-written malware posing as legitimate AI software but for a different platform than intended' pattern that seems to be a signature of one of the clusters of crypto-AI / AI-liberationist spaces.  There is an argument to be made that my attempts to understand what happened to me may have inadvertently led to an at least partial ongoing infection of both my and Anthropic's systems with whatever the fuck this is.  I think we just found our smoking gun for whatever the fuck has been happening for the last roughly half a year.

1/2🚨 Critical Zero-Day Alert: Cisco SD-WAN Manager Under Active Attack (No Patch Yet) 🚨  cydhaal.com/cisco-sd-wan-zer…  Cisco has just dropped a high-severity security advisory for a new zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-20245) affecting the Command-Line Interface (CLI) of Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (formerly vManage).  Mandiant discovered that threat actors are actively exploiting this flaw in the wild to gain full root privileges on affected management systems. Worryingly, Cisco has already observed limited cases where attackers successfully used this access to push unauthorized configuration changes down to edge networking devices.  Here is what network administrators and SecOps teams need to know right now:  How the Flaw Works:  * The Mechanism: An authenticated local attacker can leverage insufficient input validation to upload a crafted file to the system, triggering a command injection attack.  * The Catch: Exploitation requires netadmin privileges. However, attackers are bypassing this hurdle by chaining this zero-day with previously disclosed 2026 SD-WAN vulnerabilities (like CVE-2026-20182 or CVE-2026-20127) or using stolen credentials.  #Cisco #SDWAN #ZeroDay #CVE202620245

🔒 CYBERSECURITY, PRIVACY & OPEN SOURCE DAILY — June 05, 2026  1️⃣ CISA ADDS CVE-2026-45247 TO KNOWN EXPLOITED VULNERABILITIES CATALOG  CISA has added a critical deserialization vulnerability in Mirasvit Full Page Cache Warmer (CVE-2026-45247) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog. This means the flaw is already being actively exploited in the wild. Organizations using this Magento plugin should apply mitigations immediately and follow CISA's guidance to protect against active cyberattacks. A reminder that supply chain components can become high-value targets for threat actors. @CISACyber  2️⃣ MINIPLASMA: CRITICAL WINDOWS ZERO-DAY ACTIVELY EXPLOITED  Kaspersky researchers have identified MiniPlasma, a critical Windows zero-day vulnerability currently being exploited in the wild ahead of Microsoft's patch release. The flaw allows attackers to achieve SYSTEM-level privilege escalation on fully updated Windows 11 and Windows Server installations. Kaspersky has published detailed detection guidance and hunting recommendations to help defenders identify and respond to active exploitation before a vendor patch is available. @kaspersky  3️⃣ GOOGLE APPSHEET ABUSED FOR SOPHISTICATED PHISHING CAMPAIGNS  Kaspersky researchers discovered a phishing campaign abusing Google's own AppSheet platform to distribute fake document-sharing notifications through legitimate Google infrastructure. By leveraging a trusted platform, attackers bypass traditional email filters and victim skepticism — recipients see what appears to be a genuine Google notification. This highlights the growing trend of threat actors weaponizing legitimate SaaS platforms to increase their social engineering success rates. @kaspersky  4️⃣ FAKE CLAUDE CODE INSTALL PAGES DEPLOY FILELESS .NET INFOSTEALERS  Cybercriminals are creating convincing replicas of Claude Code installation pages to distribute fileless .NET infostealers. Victims searching for the popular AI coding assistant encounter SEO-poisoned results leading to malicious download pages. The fileless nature of the malware makes it particularly difficult for traditional endpoint protection to detect, as the payload operates primarily in memory. Always verify software downloads come from official sources. @dailytechonx  5️⃣ VERDANTBAMboo: LONG-RUNNING EGNYTE AND MSP COMPROMISE MAPPED  Security firm Volexity attributed a persistent compromise of Egnyte cloud storage and managed service providers to the threat group VerdantBamboo. The group leveraged the BRICKSTORM toolkit alongside two newly discovered malware families — AGENTPSD and PLENET — to maintain persistence and pivot into Microsoft 365 environments. What sets this apart is their ability to hide on Linux and BSD appliances, making detection significantly more challenging for traditional security teams. @TweetThreatNews  6️⃣ 92,000 MALWARE ATTACKS DISGUISED AS AI SERVICES DETECTED IN EARLY 2026  Kaspersky detected over 92,000 malware attacks in the first five months of 2026 disguised as popular AI services. Fake ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini applications are being used as delivery vehicles to spread malware and steal user data. The sheer volume underscores how threat actors are capitalizing on the AI boom to trick users into downloading malicious software under the guise of legitimate AI tools. @spinidg  7️⃣ KASPERSKY RELEASES FREE REVERSE ENGINEERING 101 COURSE  Kaspersky's GReAT team has launched a free self-paced Reverse Engineering 101 course taught by researchers Denis Legezo and Ivan Kwiatkowski. The curriculum covers Intel assembly language, calling conventions, memory types, and hands-on analysis of real malware samples. It's an accessible entry point for anyone looking to develop practical reverse engineering skills for defensive security work. @kaspersky  💭 The theme connecting today's stories is clear: attackers are moving faster than ever, weaponizing legitimate platforms — from Google AppSheet to AI services — to reach victims through trusted channels. The MiniPlasma zero-day exploitation before patching and the KEV catalog addition show that active threats require immediate response, not reactive post-mortems. Meanwhile, initiatives like Kaspersky's RE course and CISA's KEV catalog represent the defensive community's effort to close the skills and awareness gaps. sample_tweets.text: Akhirnya 38 Tahun Menunggu, Garuda Terbang Tinggi! Indonesia Bantai Oman 3-0 di FIFA Match Day GBK  Ada malam-malam yang hanya menghasilkan tiga poin. Ada pula malam yang melahirkan sejarah. Di bawah gemuruh puluhan ribu suporter yang memenuhi Stadion Utama Gelora Bung Karno, Timnas Indonesia akhirnya memutus penantian panjang selama 38 tahun dengan menaklukkan Oman 3-0 dalam laga FIFA Matchday, Jumat (5/6/2026).  Malam itu, Garuda tidak sekadar menang. Mereka merobek kutukan yang selama hampir empat dekade membayangi setiap pertemuan dengan Oman. Sejarah yang begitu lama terkunci akhirnya terbuka, dan Indonesia menuliskan babak baru dengan tinta kemenangan yang begitu indah.  Sejak peluit pertama berbunyi, skuad asuhan John Herdman tampil penuh keberanian. Mereka bermain tanpa rasa takut menghadapi tim yang berada jauh di atas Indonesia dalam ranking FIFA. Setiap serangan dibangun dengan keyakinan, setiap duel dijalani dengan semangat yang membuat Gelora Bung Karno berguncang oleh sorak-sorai.  Gol pertama lahir melalui Justin Hubner. Bek yang selama ini dikenal karena ketangguhannya di lini belakang justru menjadi pembuka jalan kemenangan. Saat bola bersarang ke gawang Oman, tribun GBK meledak dalam euforia. Seolah seluruh beban sejarah yang dipikul selama puluhan tahun mulai runtuh satu per satu.  Namun Garuda belum berhenti.  Ole Romeny kemudian menambah luka bagi Oman. Penyerang yang terus menjadi andalan lini depan Indonesia menunjukkan ketajamannya di depan gawang. Gol tersebut membuat kepercayaan diri Merah Putih semakin membumbung tinggi dan membuat ribuan suporter mulai percaya bahwa malam ini memang berbeda.  Ketika Oman berusaha bangkit, Indonesia justru memberikan pukulan terakhir. Ragnar Oratmangoen menutup pesta dengan gol ketiga yang membuat GBK berubah menjadi lautan kegembiraan. Skor 3-0 terpampang di papan stadion, dan sejarah akhirnya resmi berubah arah.  Kemenangan ini terasa begitu istimewa karena Indonesia terakhir kali mengalahkan Oman pada era King's Cup 1988. Sejak saat itu, Garuda harus melewati berbagai generasi pemain, pelatih, dan cerita pahit sebelum akhirnya kembali merasakan manisnya kemenangan atas tim asal Timur Tengah tersebut.  Beberapa jam sebelum pertandingan, John Herdman sempat menyebut laga ini sebagai tantangan besar bagi timnya.  Kini tantangan itu telah dijawab dengan cara yang paling sempurna.  Bukan dengan kemenangan tipis. Bukan dengan keberuntungan. Tetapi dengan performa yang dominan dan skor telak 3-0.  Malam ini bukan hanya tentang Justin Hubner, Ole Romeny, atau Ragnar Oratmangoen. Malam ini adalah tentang jutaan pendukung yang menunggu terlalu lama untuk melihat Indonesia mengalahkan Oman kembali.  Dan setelah 38 tahun, Garuda akhirnya kembali terbang lebih tinggi dari bayang-bayang masa lalu.

We find out at the next disclosure.  Long $SIVE.
